title: KR Draft Excerpt Record
description: Schema for a draft excerpt record — the output of the excerpting engine (محرك الاقتطاف) consumed by the taxonomy engine (محرك التصنيف). An excerpt is a self-contained unit of knowledge: independently understandable, attributed, and classified to a proposed taxonomy leaf. This schema defines the 'draft' lifecycle stage per §2.4. Source-agnostic: operates below the normalization boundary.

excerpt_id.description: Globally unique identifier: {book_id}:exc:{six_digit_sequence}. Immutable once assigned.

passage_id.description: ID of the passage this excerpt was extracted from.

science_id.description: Which science tree this excerpt is destined for. Inherited from passage science_id or determined by the excerpting engine for multi-science sources. Required per §7.3.

source_layer.description: Source layer. All atoms in an excerpt must share the same source_layer.

excerpt_kind.description: Primary classification. teaching: scholarly content. exercise: practice material. apparatus: editorial/structural content.

atom_ids.description: Ordered list of atom IDs that compose this excerpt. Includes both core and context atoms.

core_atom_ids.description: Atom IDs that substantively address the excerpt's taxonomy topic.

role.description: Functional role of the context atom (e.g., 'evidence', 'classification_frame', 'prerequisite').

context_atom_ids.description: Atoms providing essential framing but not part of the core teaching. Each has a typed role.

excerpt_title.description: Human-readable title for this excerpt. Must be unique among excerpts under the same proposed_leaf within a source.

excerpt_title_reason.description: Short justification of how excerpt_title was formed. Training signal for future automation.

proposed_leaf.description: The taxonomy leaf where this excerpt is proposed to be placed. PROPOSED at draft stage — the taxonomy engine may adjust during placement.

taxonomy_version.description: Version identifier of the taxonomy tree at excerpt creation time. Enables migration tracking when the taxonomy evolves.

taxonomy_path.description: Human-readable breadcrumb trail from taxonomy root to proposed_leaf. Informational — authoritative placement is proposed_leaf.

school.description: Scholarly school (مذهب) represented in this excerpt, if applicable. null if the science has no schools or school is not determinable for this excerpt. Per §2.4, school affiliation is per-excerpt, not per-author.

self_containment_score.description: Score indicating how independently understandable this excerpt is, per §5.1's self-containment requirement. 1.0 = fully self-contained. Lower scores indicate dependency on external context.

lifecycle_stage.description: Excerpt lifecycle stage. Always 'draft' at this schema boundary — becomes 'reviewed' after human gate, 'placed' after taxonomy writes to library, per §2.4.

primary_text.description: The complete verbatim text of the excerpt — concatenation of constituent atoms' text. This is the text integrity invariant target per §5.1: excerpt primary text is never modified after creation.

heading_path.description: Ordered heading atom IDs forming the navigational path. Heading atoms are structural metadata, not teaching content.

boundary_reasoning.description: Explanation of why this excerpt starts and ends where it does. Audit trail for human reviewers and training signal.

case_types.description: Pattern annotations documenting which excerpting patterns appear (e.g., 'A1_pure_definition', 'B1_clean_boundary'). Training labels for the extraction system.

relations.description: Typed directional links to other excerpts.

cross_science_context.description: True when excerpt content relies on concepts from another science.

related_science.description: Which other science is involved when cross_science_context is true. Null otherwise.

interwoven_group_id.description: Non-null when multiple excerpts share inseparable text covering multiple taxonomy topics. All excerpts in the group share this UUID.

metadata_extensions.description: Open object for science-specific metadata per §5.4. Schema is open because extensions are defined per-science in Level 3 documentation. Examples: exercise-specific fields (exercise_role, tests_nodes), split_discussion tracking.
